subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, WFP/OCHA, V-Dem, Freedom House, RSF, TI, ISS) + international press; near-term political confidence reduced given the recency of the Oct 2025 coup and an unsettled transition.

An October 2025 CAPSAT-led military coup (after a Gen Z protest wave over chronic power/water cuts) ousted President Rajoelina; an AU-suspended interim government under Col. Randrianirina took office.

Standout drags: a structural-poverty outlier (~80% under $2.15/day with no major war), chronic state failure (JIRAMA blackouts), a fresh coup, and a Grand Sud drought/famine emergency (~1.7M affected).

economic.notes: An IMF-programmed but very-low-revenue economy with a narrow export base and post-coup execution risk.

fiscal_state.notes: On an IMF ECF/RSF with a narrowing deficit but a very low revenue base and post-coup execution risk. (IMF Country Report 25/221)

monetary_independence.notes: A managed-float ariary with a thin central bank - limited but real autonomy, undermined by shallow markets and FX scarcity. (IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The ariary is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: ~8.6% projected to ease toward ~6% - moderate-to-elevated and food-price-sensitive. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: Small, shallow, low-penetration; no acute crisis but limited depth. (IMF; World Bank)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A narrow export base (vanilla, nickel/cobalt via Ambatovy ~32% of FX) vulnerable to price shocks, with a widening current account. (IMF; World Bank)

institutional.notes: Weak courts, chronic under-resourcing, and a restrictive media code.

rule_of_law.notes: Weak judicial independence and entrenched corruption (CPI ~26/100). (TI; Freedom House)

statistical_integrity.notes: IMF-engaged on macro data but thin statistical capacity. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Chronically under-resourced with poor delivery - the JIRAMA failures that triggered the 2025 uprising exemplify it. (World Bank; Al Jazeera)

press_freedom.notes: Restrictive media-code provisions (fake-news fines, defamation) and periodic repression. (RSF)

political_social.notes: A fresh coup and AU suspension over a chronically volatile, deeply poor baseline.

political_stability.notes: An Oct 2025 coup ousted Rajoelina; an AU-suspended, military-led interim with an undefined transition - acute near-term uncertainty. (Al Jazeera; ISS)

civil_liberties.notes: 'Partly Free' (FH 55), with a Sept-Oct 2025 crackdown that killed at least ~22. (Freedom House; Al Jazeera)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Not a major migration destination or refugee host - largely a non-issue with limited data. (inference)

social_cohesion.notes: Deep poverty and a coastal/highland cleavage, plus dahalo banditry, but no large-scale ethnic conflict. (Freedom House; ICG)

demographics.notes: Young and fast-growing - a potential dividend but a strain given mass poverty and weak human capital. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: Non-aligned but post-coup AU-suspended, with no major conflict.

alliance_reliability.notes: Generally non-aligned; the post-coup AU suspension and France's role in evacuating Rajoelina inject friction. (AU; ISS)

conflict_involvement.notes: No interstate or major insurgent conflict; internal insecurity is banditry/protest, not war. (ACLED; ICG)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Not broadly sanctioned; FX shortages and import constraints but no major formal controls. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Among the most cyclone/drought-exposed states, with a chronic power crisis and weak health.

climate.notes: Among the world's most cyclone-exposed - recurrent cyclones plus a southern near-famine drought, consistently high Germanwatch CRI, low readiness. (Germanwatch CRI; ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: Very weak, with high malnutrition and low coverage especially in the south. (WHO; World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: A chronic electricity crisis (JIRAMA blackouts >8h/day that sparked the 2025 protests), poor roads, low electrification. (Al Jazeera; World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Urban crime plus rural dahalo banditry, elevated post-coup. (OSAC; Freedom House)

summary: A structural-poverty outlier - among the world's poorest by income despite never fighting a major war - where chronic state failure, exemplified by years of JIRAMA blackouts, boiled over into a Gen Z protest movement and an October 2025 CAPSAT military coup that ousted Andry Rajoelina and installed an AU-suspended interim government. The economy has thin offsets (an IMF ECF/RSF program, moderating inflation, Ambatovy nickel/cobalt and vanilla earnings) but a narrow export base and a widening current account. Physical risk is severe: relentless cyclones and a Grand Sud drought-and-famine emergency affecting roughly 1.7 million. The near-term political picture is acutely uncertain; confidence is reduced by the freshness of the coup.
